screen.gradlemc.title: GradleMC
screen.gradlemc.subtitle: Local modpack stability and adaptive diagnostics
screen.gradlemc.nav.title: Sections
screen.gradlemc.overview.description: GradleMC collects bounded local diagnostics for modpack stability, performance context, report exports, and practical troubleshooting.
screen.gradlemc.overview.open_note: Open this panel with /gradlemc gui or the configured GradleMC keybind.
screen.gradlemc.smart_ai.explain: Adaptive diagnostics use local rule-based scoring and adaptive thresholds. They do not use generative AI, cloud AI, online inference, telemetry, embeddings, or external ML services.
screen.gradlemc.settings.read_only: These settings are shown read-only. Edit gradlemc-common.toml or use supported commands where available.
screen.gradlemc.settings.overlay_note: Overlay settings are local client preferences and are saved to the GradleMC config.
screen.gradlemc.settings.gpu_note: GPU usage is not shown unless a reliable provider exists. GradleMC only shows renderer/name metadata from OpenGL.
screen.gradlemc.quick_actions.descriptio: Run common GradleMC diagnostics from buttons. Server commands still use normal command permission checks.
screen.gradlemc.tests.note: Start buttons are disabled while the matching bounded test is already running. Stop buttons use the existing safe stop paths.
screen.gradlemc.profiler.description: Run bounded local profiling sessions with tick timelines, Java CPU-lite sampling, and memory/GC pressure signals.
screen.gradlemc.profiler.warning: Advanced all-thread or very low interval sampling can add overhead. CPU-lite is Java stack sampling, not async-profiler. Memory-lite is pressure tracking, not allocation profiling.
screen.gradlemc.about.description: GradleMC provides local diagnostics, stability checks, adaptive insights, and lightweight reports for Minecraft modpacks.
screen.gradlemc.about.ai_note: Adaptive diagnostics in GradleMC means transparent local scoring and adaptive thresholds, not an LLM or generative AI system.
screen.gradlemc.status.enter_modid: Enter a mod ID to search
screen.gradlemc.status.opened_folder: Opened the local GradleMC output folder
screen.gradlemc.status.open_folder_faile: Could not open the local output folder
screen.gradlemc.status.no_latest_path: No latest output path is available
screen.gradlemc.tooltip.refresh: Refresh synced dashboard state from the server.
screen.gradlemc.tooltip.mod_search: Run /gradlemc mods search with the entered mod ID.
screen.gradlemc.tooltip.entities: Run a bounded nearby entity scan.
screen.gradlemc.tooltip.block_entities: Run a bounded nearby block entity scan.
screen.gradlemc.tooltip.open_folder: Open the local GradleMC output folder.
screen.gradlemc.tooltip.copy_path: Copy the latest known output path.
screen.gradlemc.tooltip.stop: Stop the active bounded sample using the existing command path.
screen.gradlemc.profiler.tooltip.mode: Choose tick, cpu-lite, memory-lite, or combined profiling.
screen.gradlemc.profiler.tooltip.interva: Cycle the Java stack sampling interval. Lower values cost more.
screen.gradlemc.profiler.tooltip.thread: Choose server thread, render thread, all threads, or a custom pattern.
screen.gradlemc.profiler.tooltip.thresho: Cycle the slow tick threshold used for snapshots.
screen.gradlemc.profiler.tooltip.slow_on: When on, CPU-lite samples are committed only around slow ticks.
screen.gradlemc.profiler.tooltip.sleepin: Include waiting or sleeping threads in Java stack samples.
screen.gradlemc.profiler.tooltip.start: Start a bounded local profiler session.
screen.gradlemc.profiler.tooltip.stop: Stop and write TXT/JSON profile reports.
screen.gradlemc.profiler.tooltip.summary: Show the latest profile summary in chat.
screen.gradlemc.profiler.tooltip.latest: Show the latest profile report path.
screen.gradlemc.profiler.tooltip.open: Show the local GradleMC profiles folder.
screen.gradlemc.tooltip.overlay_setting: Toggle or cycle this local overlay setting.
screen.gradlemc.command.gui: Open the GradleMC control and status panel.
screen.gradlemc.command.status: Show a compact status summary in chat.
screen.gradlemc.command.ai_status: Show adaptive diagnostics state for the current player.
screen.gradlemc.command.ai_reset: Reset adaptive diagnostics runtime data for the current player. Requires permission level 2.
command.gradlemc.gui.player_only: GradleMC GUI can only be opened by an in-game player.
